subject: DevOps
title: Cloudflare's Regex Outage (2019)
description: A quick, practical guide to Cloudflare's Regex Outage (2019).

hook.text: July 2, 2019. Cloudflare went down globally. CPU usage hit 100%. The cause: one bad regex.

buildup.text: A WAF rule update contained a regex with catastrophic backtracking. It consumed all CPU.

discovery.text: The regex was deployed globally at once with no canary phase. Every edge server was hit.

twist.text: They couldn't push a fix because the deployment system used the same network that was down.

climax.text: Engineers walked to the data center and applied the fix manually. Downtime: 27 minutes.

punchline.text: Test regex carefully. One bad pattern can take down a CDN.

quiz.question: What caused Cloudflare's 2019 global outage?
options: A regular expression with catastrophic backtracking
A power failure at their main data center
